zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Brandon於大約1425年出生在英國素福爾可望福德時，他的父親Robert Brandon26歲，母親Ada Calthorpe22歲。他與Elizabeth Wingfield在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他於1425年被列為knight marshal of marshalsea。他去世於1491年4月9日之後在英國，埋葬在英國素瑞素瓦可。

nameMeanings.name: Brandon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Brandon, in Durham, Norfolk, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom, gorse’ + dūn ‘hill’. One in Lincolnshire, however, may be named after the Brant River, on which it stands; Ekwall derives the river name from Old English brant ‘steep’, presumably with reference to its steep banks. This surname was known in Ireland early enough to have a Gaelicized form de Breandún.
Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breandáin ‘son of Breandán’, a rare Kerry surname from the Latinized form of the name of the local saint, Brendan (Brendanus from Bréanainn).
French: from the Old French oblique case of the personal name Brando, of ancient Germanic origin (see Brand 1).

variants.name: Brand
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, Scandinavian, Dutch, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from the ancient Germanic personal name Brando, Old Norse Brandr, a short form of various compound names containing the element brand ‘sword’ (a derivative of brinnan ‘to flash’), of which the best known is Hildebrand . There is placename evidence for Brant(a) as an Old English personal name; however, the Middle English personal name Brand was probably introduced to England from Old Norse.
German: variant of   Brandt   1 and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Swedish and Danish: ornamental name or nickname from brand ‘fire’.

variants.name: Brandom
meanings: English and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Brandon  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Brandon was born about 1425, in Wangford, Suffolk, England, his father, Robert Brandon, was 26 and his mother, Ada Calthorpe, was 22. He married Elizabeth Wingfield in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 9 daughters. In 1425, his occupation is listed as knight marshal of marshalsea. He died after 9 April 1491, in England, and was buried in Southwark, Surrey, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Brandon est né environ 1425, à Wangford, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert Brandon, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Ada Calthorpe, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Wingfield à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 9 filles. En 1425, sa profession de son indiquée est knight marshal of marshalsea. Il est décédé après 9 avril 1491, à Angleterre, et a été enterré à Southwark, Surrey, Angleterre.

Lui sposato Elizabeth Wingfield nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 9 figlie. Nel 1425, la sua occupazione è indicata come knight marshal of marshalsea. Morì dopo il 9 aprile 1491, nel England, e fu sepolto a Southwark, Surrey, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Brandonが約1425年にイギリススフォルクワングフォードで生まれた時，父Robert Brandonは26歳，母Ada Calthorpeは22歳であった。イギリスでElizabeth Wingfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。1425年，職業はknight marshal of marshalseaと記録されている。彼は1491年4月9日以後にイギリスで死去し，イギリススリーソウスワークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Brandon(이)가 대략 1425년 영국 수폴크 왕퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Brandon(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada Calthorpe(은)는 22세였다. 그는 영국에서 Elizabeth Wingfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 1425년, 그의 직업은 knight marshal of marshalsea이었다. 그는 1491년 4월 9일 이후 영국에서 사망했으며 영국 수리 소우스워크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Brandon nasceu aproximadamente 1425, em Wangford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Brandon, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Ada Calthorpe, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Wingfield em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 9 filhas. Em 1425, sua profissão aparece como knight marshal of marshalsea. Ele faleceu após 9 de abril de 1491, em Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Southwark, Surrey, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Brandon родился около 1425 г. в местности вангфорд, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Brandon было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada Calthorpe, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Wingfield в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 9 дочерей. В 1425 году он работал по профессии knight marshal of marshalsea. Он умер после 9 апрель 1491 г. в местности Англия и был похоронен в местности соутварк, сурри, Англия.
